name: bolt
title: bolt
summary: system daemon to manage thunderbolt 3 devices
description:
system daemon to manage thunderbolt 3 devices
Thunderbolt 3 features different security modes that require
devices to be authorized before they can be used. The D-Bus API can be
used to list devices, enroll them (authorize and store them in the
local database) and forget them again (remove previously enrolled
devices). It also emits signals if new devices are connected (or
removed). During enrollment devices can be set to be automatically
authorized as soon as they are connected.  A command line tool, called
boltctl, can be used to control the daemon and perform all the above
mentioned tasks.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use boltctl because system daemon to manage thunderbolt 3 devices.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use boltctl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `boltctl authorize DEVICE` as documented upstream for boltctl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `boltctl config` as documented upstream for boltctl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `boltctl domains` as documented upstream for boltctl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `boltctl enroll DEVICE` as documented upstream for boltctl.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair boltctl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `boltctl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install bolt. system daemon to manage thunderbolt 3 devices.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: system daemon to manage thunderbolt 3 devices.

usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install bolt
Install with: sudo apt install bolt
